channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Inside a Coast Guard border crackdown
item.description: Chief Peter Nelson, officer in charge for Coast Guard Station San Diego, described escalating confrontations with smugglers.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's nominee to be the next Joint Chiefs chairman will face senators' questions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s pick to be the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan “Razin” Caine, will face questions from senators during his confirmation hearing Tuesday about his qualifications to become the top U.S. military officer.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Senate vote to reverse Trump's tariffs on Canada is testing Republican support
item.description: With President Donald Trump’s so-called “Liberation Day” of tariff implementation fast approaching, Senate Democrats are putting Republican support for some of those plans to the test by forcing a vote to nullify the emergency declaration that underpins the tariffs on Canada.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The key places to watch in Tuesday's elections in Wisconsin and Florida
item.description: A handful of elections in Florida and Wisconsin have become key tests of President Donald Trump’s political standing just over two months into his second term in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Robert Yoon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump admin withholds millions from Planned Parenthood for civil rights and executive order violations: report
item.description: The Trump administration has reportedly withheld millions of dollars from Planned Parenthood affiliates for violating civil rights laws and executive orders against DEI programs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court candidates make final push amid high spending, voting
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — President Donald Trump's preferred candidate for Wisconsin Supreme Court and his Democratic-backed challenger made a final blitz across the state on Monday, the day before voting concludes in a race where early turnout has surged and spending is nearing $100 million.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beware of phones being checked returning back to US: Attorney
item.description: U.S. citizens may face questions if they're traveling with visaholders or living with individuals who are in the U.S. illegally, Nasseri says.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: EPA chief Zeldin says he is closing the agency's one-room museum, saving taxpayers $600,000 a year
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin says he is closing a one-room museum at the agency's Washington headquarters, saving taxpayers $600,000 a year in operating costs.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic base's anger puts some party leaders on shaky ground
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The Democratic base is angry. Not just at President Donald Trump, Elon Musk and the “Make America Great Again” movement. Rank-and-file Democrats are mad at their own leaders and increasingly agitating to replace them. Democrats in Arizona and Georgia pushed out their party chairs.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee bill to requires schools to teach ‘success sequence’ of life path
item.description: The "Success Sequence Act" would require schools to teach the positive outcomes associated with completing the following in the following order: graduate high school, enter the workforce or go to college, get married, and then have children.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: Session crossroads: Deadlines loom, budget decisions near, sports stadium requests simmer
item.description: Legislators will mull over requests for sports facility upgrades, as they get a clearer idea for the budget they’ll be working with.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Gov. DeSantis proposes $1K rebate checks to cover Florida homeowners' property tax
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a proposal on Monday that would give Florida homeowners a rebate averaging $1,000 for each homesteaded property in the state.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 6 things borrowers should know about federal student loans right now
item.description: NPR has spent the past few weeks catching up with student loan experts and asking the Trump administration for clarity on some of borrowers’ biggest questions.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: White House to remove magnolia tree dating to Andrew Jackson
item.description: Trump added that White House staff will preserve the Magnolia tree’s “Historic wood,” which, Trump said, “may be used for other high and noble purposes!!!”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Media questions can shape Trump's decisions in second term
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Top Minnesota election official says Trump is attempting ‘unlawful’ power grab
item.description: Minnesota Secretary of State Steve Simon said President Donald Trump’s recent executive order targeting elections was “unlawful.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Illinois to defy Trump voter order for April election
item.description: President Trump earlier this week issued an executive order calling for all voters to provide proof of citizenship before receiving a ballot.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Wisconsin reporter gives insight on country’s most expensive judicial race
item.description: More than $90 million has poured into a Wisconsin Supreme Court race that will decide the court’s ideological majority.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union and Democracy Forward sued the Trump administration over its use of the wartime law to quickly deport people, which they say violates due process.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: DFLers at Minnesota Capitol rip Musk’s ideas while the GOP wants a closer look
item.description: Billionaire and top Trump adviser Elon Musk is reshaping the federal government. In Minnesota, Democrats are increasingly turning Musk mentions into a punching bag. And Republicans say his work could be replicated in St. Paul.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers to make Trump White House visit next month
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers plan to visit President Donald Trump at the White House to celebrate their World Series victory, the team announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: FBI launches Tesla vandalism task force
item.description: The FBI confirmed Monday it has established a task force to investigate the wave of vandalism and arson incidents targeting Tesla in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: US infrastructure improved with Biden-era spending but there's a long way to go
item.description: A report card on the upkeep of America's infrastructure gave it a “C” grade on Tuesday, up slightly from previous years.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5
